meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Overcoming No SaaS Knowledge for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with SaaS architecture. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship high-converting apps in record time.
content.answer_block: Eliminate the technical barrier by leveraging a pre-configured SaaS starter kit. By combining Antigravity’s structural generation with Claude Code’s autonomous implementation, you bypass months of boilerplate learning. This stack allows you to ship production-ready authentication, database schemas, and billing logic in days, not months, while maintaining professional-grade standards.
content.intro:
The cold sweat of a client asking for a custom SaaS dashboard when you've only ever built static landing pages is a rite of passage every freelancer dreads. You know the design, but the backend architecture—the authentication flows, the Stripe webhooks, the database migrations—feels like a labyrinth designed to drain your sanity and your profit margins.

But the era of manual boilerplate is dead. Today, we bridge the gap between 'designing a UI' and 'shipping a SaaS.' By leveraging a battle-tested starter kit coupled with AI-driven development flows, you can transform from a UI designer into a full-stack SaaS architect overnight.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project structure using a proven SaaS starter kit to ensure industry-standard security and folder organization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js 14 SaaS starter architecture with Supabase authentication, Stripe billing integration, and Tailwind CSS configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize the project structure based on the Antigravity spec.'

steps.title: Define Data Models
steps.description: Translate your client's requirements into a robust database schema without writing raw SQL migrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Prisma schema models for Users, Subscriptions, and Projects with appropriate relational mapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the defined Prisma schema and run migrations.'

steps.title: Configure Auth & Security
steps.description: Secure your application endpoints by implementing role-based access control (RBAC) at the framework level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Next-Auth with Github and Google providers, ensuring middleware protection for internal routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Secure all dashboard routes using the configured middleware.'

steps.title: Integrate Billing Logic
steps.description: Hook up Stripe webhooks to handle subscription lifecycles, ensuring your client gets paid automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Stripe webhook handler for subscription.created and invoice.payment_succeeded events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Connect Stripe webhooks to the database user update flow.'

steps.title: Deploy & Ship
steps.description: Finalize the production build pipeline to ensure zero-downtime deployments via Vercel or your preferred provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up environment variables template and production deployment configuration for Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Verify build integrity and generate deployment docs.'

pro_tips: Always enforce strict TypeScript types on your API responses to prevent runtime errors in your client's dashboard.
Use a 'Feature Flag' pattern early so you can toggle experimental modules without breaking the core production build.
Map your database relationships in a visual tool before running your Antigravity prompts to ensure the AI understands your business logic.
Automate your database seeding with mock data to test your UI flows before connecting to real user data.
Keep your Claude Code context small by breaking complex features into incremental prompts rather than one giant request.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, document your custom component library within the starter kit to create a reusable 'SaaS design system' for your future freelance clients.
content.conversion_block: Stop reinventing the wheel and start shipping value. By mastering this workflow, you reclaim your billable hours and establish yourself as a premium SaaS developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
